Let me read the handoffs and check if there's a baseline to diff against.
The extension is untracked so there's no git baseline. Let me read the actual source files to review the implementation.
Now let me read index.ts, cadence.ts, memory.ts, and the tests.
Now let me read memory.ts (refreshIndex/selectDeltaTranscripts) and the two test scripts.
Now let me read the cadence test and README.
Let me check runner.ts for the `RunUpdaterResult` type and the updater crew doc that was mentioned as changed.
Let me verify the transcript types and digest functions used in the changed code.
Let me verify there are no other call sites of the changed function signatures and check that the tests actually pass as claimed.
Let me verify the `install.sh` change (showed as modified) and confirm it's not unrelated scope creep, plus check the full learn_test line numbers cited by the tester.
The whole `continual-learning` extension is untracked, and `install.sh` shows a Foreman-touching change. Let me determine whether `install.sh` belongs to this task and verify the test assertions line up.
Let me verify no stale references to the old behavior remain (120 default, pre-write, old return type) and check the skill doc.
The 120 in cadence_test.sh:19 is the intentional explicit `opts()` value (task said to keep it). Let me check SKILL.md for stale cadence references and verify the index.ts notify logic edge cases.
I have thoroughly reviewed all task files. Let me do a final check on the README "Files" section reference and confirm the test assertions align with the implementation.
I've completed a thorough review of all task deliverables. Since the `continual-learning` extension is entirely untracked, `git diff` only surfaces `install.sh`; I reviewed the actual three-fix source files directly.

## Verification of the three fixes

**Fix 1 (learn.ts) â€” only mark mined transcripts** âś“
- `buildUpdaterTask` returns `{ task, included }` (learn.ts:117-141), caps at 8 transcripts AND 60k chars, and the `included.length > 0 &&` guard guarantees the first transcript is always included even when oversized (learn.ts:122).
- `runLearningPass` refreshes the index for `included` only and sets `deltaCount = included.length` (learn.ts:214-219). Over-cap deltas correctly drain on subsequent passes.

**Fix 2 (learn.ts + index.ts) â€” failure doesn't advance index, is surfaced** âś“
- Index write moved after the updater and gated on `result.exitCode === 0` (learn.ts:210-214); the backwards pre-write is gone. Thrown updater also returns `ok:false` without writing (learn.ts:200-203).
- `LearnRunOutcome` extended with `ok`/`stderr`; `updaterFailureReason` folds a 1000-char stderr tail into `reason` (learn.ts:163-166).
- Both index.ts paths warn on `outcome.ran && !outcome.ok` (index.ts:92, 116) and the success guard now requires `outcome.ok` (index.ts:94). `ensureLearnedScaffold` still runs pre-updater (learn.ts:191).

**Fix 3 (cadence.ts + README) â€” cadence fires in normal use** âś“
- `DEFAULT_MIN_MINUTES = 30`, `DEFAULT_MIN_TURNS = 10` (cadence.ts:51-52); AND semantics and trial logic untouched. README says "30 minutes". cadence_test.sh default assertion â†’ 30; the minutes-gate test keeps explicit `opts({minMinutes:120})` so it stays valid.

**Tests** â€” learn_test.sh adds genuine coverage: exit-42 stub does NOT advance index (line ~88), retry advances on success, 9-transcript batching drains 8â†’1, oversized single transcript still `included.length===1`. Not weakened.

No security issues (stderr is length-bounded and only displayed). Error handling is correct at the real boundary (updater throw caught, exitCode checked, outer wrapper try/catch). The `crew/agents-memory-updater.md` edit is a one-line contract clarification consistent with Fix 2 â€” minimal and faithful.

The `install.sh` change (the only git-tracked edit) predates this task by ~4.5h (mtime 11:16 vs 15:57-16:00) and is the extension's install scaffolding (per-file crew/skill linking so the new extension's subagent installs); the Foreman crew agents still link via the new loop, so it's behavior-preserving for Foreman. Not part of the three fixes and not a regression.

NITS
- README.md:112 â€” Files section still labels the export `runForemanLearningPass`, but the actual export is `runLearningPassForCwd` (index.ts:46). Pre-existing stale doc; worth fixing since README was already touched this task.
- install.sh is staged in the working tree alongside the (untracked) extension and touches the Foreman crew-linking line; confirm it's committed with the extension rather than this bug-fix changeset.

REVIEW: APPROVE
